title: AI-written contracts - enforceability questions lawyers should prepare for
p1:
A contract drafted in seconds by AI may look perfect. But can it actually protect the parties when a dispute reaches court?

Many assume that if an AI tool creates a legally worded agreement, the document carries the same weight as one carefully prepared by a lawyer.
p2:
The surprising part: the biggest issue may not be the wording itself. It is who is responsible for the terms, whether the parties understood them, and whether the agreement reflects genuine consent.

An AI-generated clause can sound professional while hiding ambiguity, missing key obligations, or relying on outdated assumptions.
p3:
Lawyers will increasingly need to examine the process behind AI-assisted contracts, not just the final document. Questions around review, human oversight, accuracy, and accountability may become central in future disputes.

The practical lesson is simple: AI can help draft contracts, but enforceability still depends on careful legal judgment and informed agreement by the parties.
conclusion: Should courts treat AI-assisted contracts differently from traditionally drafted agreements, or should the focus remain only on the final terms and consent of the parties?
